title: FABMISR (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for FABMISR in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is FABMISR?
faq.answer: FABMISR is one of the largest foreign banks operating in Egypt and a subsidiary of First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB), the largest bank in the United Arab Emirates. The bank provides comprehensive banking products and services to all customer segments, including large organizations, SMEs, and retail consumers. Operating through 72 branches across Egypt, FABMISR leverages its parent bank's global expertise and financial strength to support the local economy, entrepreneurs, and stakeholders. The bank was formed through the legal merger of FAB Egypt and Bank Audi Egypt, combining decades of banking experience in the Egyptian market.

faq.question: When was FABMISR founded?
faq.answer: FAB's presence in Egypt dates back to 1975, when the bank opened its first branch outside the UAE in Egypt. This marked the beginning of nearly five decades of operations in the Egyptian market. The current entity, operating under the brand name FABMISR, was officially established in June 2022 following the completion of a legal merger between FAB Egypt and Bank Audi Egypt. FAB had acquired 100% of Bank Audi Egypt's share capital in January 2021, and after obtaining all regulatory approvals from the Central Bank of Egypt (CBE) and the General Authority for Investment & Free Zones (GAFI), the merger was finalized. The full system integration was completed in November 2022, creating one of Egypt's largest foreign banks with assets exceeding EGP 185 billion.

faq.question: Where is FABMISR headquartered?
faq.answer: FABMISR is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt, operating within the country's banking district. The bank's corporate offices are located at Block G, 84 90th Street, 143 Banks Zone in New Cairo. As a subsidiary of First Abu Dhabi Bank, FABMISR also maintains close ties with its parent company's headquarters in Abu Dhabi, UAE, at Khalifa Business Park.

faq.question: Where does FABMISR operate?
faq.answer: FABMISR operates extensively throughout Egypt with a strategically distributed branch network covering major cities and regions across the country. The bank maintains 72 branches in key locations including: Greater Cairo: Multiple branches across Downtown Cairo, Heliopolis, Maadi, New Cairo, Nasr City, Mokattam, Garden City, Abbasseya, and El Manyal. Alexandria: Branches in strategic areas including San Stefano, Sporting, Glim, Semouha, and Azarita. Giza: Presence in Dokki, Mohandeseen, 6th of October City, and El Haram. Delta Region: Branches in Tanta (El Gharbeya), El Mansoura (El Dakahleya), and Damietta. Upper Egypt: The bank maintains branches serving Upper Egypt communities. Red Sea and Tourist Areas: Operations in Hurghada, Sharm El Sheikh (Naama Bay), and El Gouna. The bank also operates over 200 ATMs nationwide, providing 24/7 access to banking services across Egypt.

faq.question: What services does FABMISR offer?
faq.answer: FABMISR provides a comprehensive range of banking services tailored to meet the needs of different customer segments: Retail Banking: Personal accounts, savings accounts, loans, credit cards, and consumer finance solutions. Corporate Banking: Trade finance, cash management, treasury services, and corporate lending for large organizations. Commercial Banking: Specialized services for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), supporting business growth and entrepreneurship. Investment Banking: Services including mergers and acquisitions advisory, debt and equity capital markets, and project financing. Private Banking: Personalized wealth management solutions for high-net-worth individuals. Digital Banking Services: The bank has invested heavily in digital transformation, offering: • Mobile banking application with secure transactions and OTP generation • FABMISR E-Connect: A corporate online banking channel for multinationals and large corporates, launched in partnership with Mindgate • Online banking platforms enabling account management, bill payments, and transfers • "Access" platform for merchants and businesses with social media presence • Digital payment acceptance solutions including QR codes and e-commerce integration Payment Services: Partnership with Noon Payments to provide cutting-edge digital payment solutions and enhance the e-commerce experience.

faq.question: What makes FABMISR unique?
faq.answer: Global Backing with Local Expertise: FABMISR combines nearly 50 years of experience in the Egyptian market with the financial strength and international expertise of FAB, one of the world's largest and safest banks. The parent bank holds ratings of Aa3 (Moody's), AA- (S&P), and AA- (Fitch), representing the strongest aggregate rating of any bank in the Middle East and North Africa region. Digital Innovation Leader: The bank has positioned itself at the forefront of Egypt's digital transformation, launching advanced platforms like FABMISR E-Connect that eliminate traditional banking complexities and enable seamless digital transactions for corporate clients. The partnership with fintech leaders like Mindgate and Noon Payments demonstrates commitment to innovative solutions. Sustainability Focus: FAB is a regional leader in sustainability and is part of the MSCI ESG Leaders Index and FTSE4Good EM. FABMISR operates under strategic pillars of Responsible Banking, Responsible Employment, Positive Social Impact, and strong Governance. Strategic Market Position: Following the merger with Bank Audi Egypt, FABMISR became one of the largest private sector banks operating in Egypt, with over EGP 185 billion in assets as of March 2022. This scale provides customers with enhanced capabilities and access to international markets. Commitment to Egypt's Vision 2030: The bank actively supports Egypt's strategic development goals, focusing on financial inclusion, empowering SMEs, and contributing to the country's position as a gateway to African markets.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is FABMISR?
faq.answer: FABMISR operates as a Universal Commercial Bank in Egypt. This classification reflects the bank's comprehensive service offering across multiple banking segments, including retail banking, corporate banking, commercial banking, and investment banking activities. As a foreign bank subsidiary, FABMISR is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt (CBE) and operates under Egyptian banking regulations while benefiting from the international standards and practices of its parent institution, First Abu Dhabi Bank. The bank serves as a bridge between the Egyptian market and international financial services, leveraging FAB's global network spanning five continents to support both local businesses seeking international expansion and international companies operating in Egypt.

faq.question: How did the merger with Bank Audi Egypt transform FABMISR?
faq.answer: The acquisition and merger with Bank Audi Egypt represented a transformative milestone for FAB's operations in Egypt. Bank Audi had established its presence in the Egyptian market in March 2006 through the acquisition of Cairo Far East Bank, growing from just three branches to over 53 branches across Egypt. When FAB acquired 100% of Bank Audi Egypt's share capital in January 2021, it added EGP 85.6 billion in assets to its existing Egyptian operations. Bank Audi Egypt had built a reputation for providing high-quality products and services across corporate banking, private banking, investment banking, retail banking, securities brokerage, and bancassurance. The merger process unfolded in stages: the legal merger was completed in June 2022 with the launch of the FABMISR brand, and the full system integration was announced in November 2022. This integration allowed customers from both legacy institutions to access services across all FABMISR branches and digital platforms, creating a unified entity with approximately EGP 187 billion in assets, 69 branches (later expanded to 72), and over 200 ATMs. The Lebanese Bank Audi Group's decision to sell its Egyptian unit was driven by regulatory requirements from the Central Bank of Lebanon following economic tensions in Lebanon, providing FAB with a strategic opportunity to significantly expand its Egyptian footprint.
